gaps: A way to retrieve priors by era or date window when Term is null (nearly all resolved SCOTUS priors are undated, so --group-by term_year buckets almost everything under 'none')
Case-name or free-text search in `fedcourts query` to find priors similar to a caption when the snapshot has no topic/judges/citations to filter on
Documentation of how corpus disposition labels (especially 'other') were derived for historical merits cases, to know what the event's granted/denied target actually means for them
helpful: `fedcourts stats --court scotus` gave a usable base-rate anchor for an otherwise information-free snapshot
`fedcourts query --disposition other` samples made the meaning of the dominant 'other' label legible (historical merits appeals)
`fedcourts paths` confirmed the output location unambiguously
notes: Snapshot for this docket carried almost no signal; the whole prediction is base-rate driven. See flags.json.
